---
output:
  xaringan::moon_reader:
    css: ["default", "extra.css"]
    lib_dir: libs
    seal: false
    nature:
      highlightStyle: github
      highlightLines: true
      countIncrementalSlides: false
      ratio: '16:9'
---

```{r, echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE}
##xaringan::inf_mr()
## For offline work: https://bookdown.org/yihui/rmarkdown/some-tips.html#working-offline
## Images not appearing? Put images folder inside the libs folder as that is the main data directory

library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(stargazer)
##library(kableExtra)
##library(modelr)

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE,
                      eval = TRUE,
                      error = FALSE,
                      message = FALSE,
                      warning = FALSE,
                      comment = NA)
```

class: slideblue

.size80[**Today's Agenda**]

<br>

.size50[.center[
Exploring the Success Stories (I)]

- Command & control regulations addressing environmental problems
]

<br>

.center[.size40[
  Justin Leinaweaver (Spring 2022)
]]

???

## Prep for Class
1. Review submitted cases
    - https://docs.google.com/document/d/1dDKvveHlHhms-TSNpxn4fOjk9tTYnjQx4sqUVJtm81E/edit?usp=sharing





---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-blue_triangles_v2.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center

class: middle

# Paper 2
.size45[
Which of the four policy design options is the "right" choice for addressing your environmental problem?

- Analyze the pros and cons of each option for your chosen problem, and
- Support all claims with evidence
]

???

Keep your eye on the next target: Paper 2.

In that paper you will be asked to evaluate all four of the approaches we study in class from the perspective of your chosen environmental problem.

### Make sense?



---

class: middle, slideblue

.size50[**Today: Command & Control Cases**]

.size45[
Find a real world example of .textblue[**this approach**] being used to .textblue[**successfully**] address an environmental problem .textblue[**similar to the one in your project**].
]

.size45[
.textblue[**Present as an argument**]: This case shows that addressing environmental problem X can be done successfully using C&C regulations.
]

???

### Does everybody have an argument to present and discuss today?

<br>

Let's review all the cases for APA format.



---

class: middle, slideblue

.size45[
This case shows that we can use a command and control regulation to address environmental problem X.

- What specific, narrow aspect of your problem does this regulation focus on?

- Which stakeholders does it target? What does it require them to do?

- What institutions support this regulation?
]

???

Take five minutes to reflect on your argument and get ready to present it to other people as answers to these three questions.

### Questions on this?

Get to it!

<br>

Ok, everybody stand up and welcome to our environmental policy cocktail party.

We will now take 25 minutes to mingle, present our cases and hear everybody elses' cases.

I don't want to see groups of three!

- Your job is to interact in pairs.
- I want focused groups each hearing one case and presenting one case.

### Questions?

Go!

*You mingle too.*
- This was super fun and you got to help steer students to being more specific, more precise



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-forest_v2.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.size60[.center[
Identify the .textblue[**characteristics**] associated with .textblue[**successful**] use of a .textblue[**command and control**] regulatory approach to environmental problem-solving]]

???

Ok, at this point you've heard MANY different cases and hopefully gotten some time to reflect on your own case argument.

Now let's try to extract lessons from all of them.

- Everybody form groups of (three? four?) and spread out around the room.

Groups compare and contrast each set of answers and help the class extract generalizable lessons to help us evaluate C&C regulations.





---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-forest_v2.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.size50[.center[
Identify the .textblue[**characteristics**] associated with .textblue[**successful**] use of a .textblue[**command and control**] regulatory approach to environmental problem-solving]]

.size50[
1) How specifically do these regulations target the problem? 
]

???

Ok, groups, first prompt.

I'm willing to bet each of your environmental problems is large and multifaceted (e.g. involves lots of stakeholders and is made worse by lots of different behaviors).

Your cases for today each offer a specific regulation that I'm betting targets a VERY narrow piece of this problem.

- e.g. Person X needs to stop doing Y
- Industry X needs to start using technology Y


### From what you just heard, how specifically do these regulations target the problem?
- *Report back and discuss*

<br>

### Based on these elements, what are the characteristics of an environmental problem that lends itself to using a C&C regulation? What kinds do not?

Collect responses *ON BOARD* and discuss: 'Problem Targeting'

- ?

(Class: 3/3/2022)
- Problem defined very narrowly
- Problem focused on sympathetic groups (WIC program designed to help women, infants and children)
- When the problem is MASSIVE or has dangers over the long time horizon (e.g. nuclear waste storage)
- Problem requires instructions of government employees/contractors rather than private individuals
- Multiple levels of oversight and enforcement



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-forest_v2.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.size50[.center[
Identify the .textblue[**characteristics**] associated with .textblue[**successful**] use of a .textblue[**command and control**] regulatory approach to environmental problem-solving]]

.size50[
2) What kinds of stakeholders do these regulations target? 

- Any carrots included or purely a stick?
]

???

Ok, groups, second prompt.

### From what you just heard, who specifically do these regulations target? How does it target them?
- *Report back and discuss*

<br>

### Based on these elements, what are the characteristics of a stakeholder that lends itself to using a C&C regulation? What kinds do not?

Collect responses *ON BOARD* and discuss: 'Stakeholder Targeting'

- ?

(Class: 3/3/2022)
- Answers ran the gamut from private to public interests.
- My conclusion was that stakeholder type doesn't appear to be a good differentiator for choosing C&C approaches over the others.



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-forest_v2.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.size50[.center[
Identify the .textblue[**characteristics**] associated with .textblue[**successful**] use of a .textblue[**command and control**] regulatory approach to environmental problem-solving]]

.size50[
3) What kinds of local institutions exist to support and/or reinforce these regulations? 
]

???

Ok, groups, final prompt.

### From what you just heard, what institutions does the regulation rely on? Are the regulations supported by institutional actors or built into established processes?
- *Report back and discuss*

<br>

### Based on these elements, what are the kinds of institutional needs to make a C&C regulation work?

Collect responses *ON BOARD* and discuss: 'Institutional Support'

- ?




---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-forest_v2.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle, center

.size55[**Best advice / things to remember when designing command and control regulations?**]

???

Bottom line time.

### In a practical sense, what guidance can we offer policy-makers considering using a C&C approach to solving environmental problems?

- Depends on balance of "dirty" vs "clean" industries in the target economy

- Must be paired with assistance

- Is there any way to do this that isn't "big government" getting bigger? Easy to say shut down coal mining and we'll retrain those workers but in reality how would this work? Train for what? Forced relocation to jobs? Government picking winners and losers?

- Proposed to discuss: C&C most realistic if changes are small OR industries carefully targeted.

- Enforcement at the individual level is hard if community doesn't buy-in

<br>

**Ok, everybody take five minutes to reflect on our work today.**

Jot down some notes that I promise you'll need when it comes time to write the next paper.

What are the pros and cons of using this approach for your specific problem?



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/11-1-Green_taxation.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: center

???

Next week we explore our next policy design, green taxes!

Make sure you consolidate and clean up your notes from this week.

You might end up making C&C central to your paper!
